testPurpose.objective: At the minimum supported three-player boundary, verify mixed-experience users can create, join, reload one identity, configure the recommended deck, make only visible legal decisions, complete one natural game, and receive an isolated and cross-tab-consistent terminal result.

successCriteria.description: All three players complete the create, join, start, play, and terminal journey through enabled visible UI controls.
Each tab proves its own identity and exact chat authorship; reloading one lobby tab preserves that identity without changing peer tabs.
The lobby visibly reflects three players, a 180-second discussion timer, and the Adapter-recommended deck before start.
Before terminal disclosure, no player tab or isolated player agent receives another player's nonpublic role or night details.
Every player tab visibly exposes the same complete Adapter-normalized terminal result.

testPurpose.recommendationRationale: The three-player minimum is a high-risk boundary for recommended-deck composition, role flow, identity mapping, and terminal normalization. One complete natural-user journey provides broad production-time confidence while leaving speech, deception, silence, belief changes, vote participation, targets, and winner unconstrained.
